_comment: Opt-in list of accounts AI Manager considers replying under (see src/triggers/ai_manager.py) -- the one deliberate exception to the 'never auto-reply under other accounts' rule. 'user_id' is optional: leave it blank and it auto-resolves from 'handle' on first use (one read call, then cached), or paste the numeric ID yourself to skip that lookup. An entry with enabled=false does nothing at all. 'times_per_day' is a hard per-account cap -- the global thresholds in config/ai_manager.json (max_replies_per_day) is the real ceiling across all accounts combined. 'reply_only' (default false): the big accounts below commonly restrict who can reply to their posts (X's tweet-level conversation-control setting), which is why replies/quote-tweets to them 403 -- smaller/mid accounts marked reply_only=true are unlikely to have that restriction, so AI Manager only ever considers replying to them, never reposting (reposting the big accounts already works fine via plain retweet).
